rulings.Case Title: MADELINE MCANDREW VS. HASSAN ROBIAH KURDI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form And Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorties; Declaration Of Chandrani Mandal In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT HASSAN KURDI'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form And Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition filed. Plaintiff McAndrew shall pay sanctions of $684 to defense counsel within 10 days of service of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JYOTHSNA TALKAD ET AL VS. 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant Dipali Shahs Financial Information
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS JYOTHSNA TALKAD, RADHAKRSHNA TALKAD, LEELAVATHY TALKAD'S Motion For Order Permitting Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant Dipali Shahs Financial Information. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. After weighing the evidence presented by both sides, the Court finds that plaintiffs have not demonstrated a substantial probability of success on their punitive damages claim as required by Civil Code section 3295(c). Jabro v. Superior Court (2002) 95 Cal. App. 4th 754. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FAST TRACK INVESTMENT COMPANY LLC A DELAWARE VS. LESLEY REED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT JOHN FITZPATRICK VANNUCCI, IN PRO PER's Motion To Set Aside Default Defendant Vannucci's motion to set aside default is GRANTED. Plaintiff filed its amended complaint on January 25, 2019, giving Vannucci until February 25 to file a responsive pleading. (CCP §471.5.) Plaintiff moved for entry of default four days later, March 1, though it knew Vannucci was actively litigating the case. Even if CRC 3.1320 applied here, the difference would be a matter of days. Quick-pitching a default is a sharp practice that the courts abjure. (See Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 855; Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 701.) Counsel are urged to meet and confer before filing any further motions and to treat one another as is becoming of officers of the court. Vannucci's responsive pleading shall be filed by April 17, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NEXTPULSE, LLC. VS. BRUNSWICK CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT BRUNSWICK CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's amended complaint is DENIED. As plaintiff aptly notes, motions to strike are disfavored and their use is to "cautious and sparing" - not as a "procedural line item veto." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.) Defendant argues that a novation superseded prior contracts between the parties, but this raises several factual and contract-interpretation issues not susceptible to resolution at the pleading stage. The same is true of defendant's disputes with the damage theories pled. The facts plaintiff pleads in its fraud claim would support punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CARLA MEDINA VS. PAPAZOTE COLE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents To Be Produced For Inspection And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Its Counsel, Paul S. Cowie, In The Amount Of $2,380.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 12, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CARLA MEDINA'S Motion To Compel Documents To Be Produced For Inspection And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Its Counsel, Paul S. Cowie, In The Amount Of $2,380.00. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Moving party should be prepared to identify and lodge with the court the particular discovery request that is the subject of the motion. The Court cannot tell from the papers and exhibits whether the motion refers to the PMK deposition notice, the Labor Code 1198.5 request, or some other demand for production of documents. The opposing party (Papazote Cole) shall be prepared to discuss why the documents (containing plaintiff's signature) sought are not within the purview of Judge Kahn's order dated February 26. The parties are encouraged to try to resolve this discovery dispute before or at the hearing, as the validity of the signature on the arbitration agreement appear to be pivotal evidence for the Court's eventual ruling on whether arbitration should be compelled. The Court is not inclined to award sanctions against either side. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
